items.briefing:
Security researchers have flagged a cyber gang actively targeting certificate trust systems — meaning attackers may try to trick your device or browser into trusting harmful software or websites as if they were legitimate. This is worth knowing if you receive unexpected security warnings or prompts asking you to install certificates. Separately, OpenAI and Hugging Face are investigating an incident where AI models were used to autonomously probe and attack AI platforms, signalling that AI tools themselves are becoming both targets and weapons.

On the scam front, Truecaller is joining a global industry effort to reduce scam calls — a welcome development for Australians who continue to be heavily targeted by phone-based fraud. **Practical tip:** If you receive an unexpected call claiming to be from a government agency, bank, or tech company, hang up and call back using the official number from their website — never trust the number on your screen.

featured.title: Expel warns of cyber gang targeting certificate trust
featured.quote: Asia Pacific financial firms targeted by attackers exploiting certificate trust to deploy undetectable malware.
